ladder.meaning: Named concept or intended observatory primitive; design exists but repeatable artifact evidence is not yet established.
ladder.minimum_evidence: Concept document, prompt, or architecture note.

ladder.meaning: Artifact exists, but the contract, inputs, or interpretation are still unstable.
ladder.minimum_evidence: At least one generated artifact or report with caveats.

ladder.meaning: Artifact has been produced from real run data and can be inspected, but coverage or closure may be partial.
ladder.minimum_evidence: Source path plus visible artifact or report.

ladder.meaning: Artifact satisfies its local validation gate for at least one run or fixture.
ladder.minimum_evidence: PASS closure/verdict or equivalent local gate.

ladder.meaning: Artifact has repeatable structure, documented interpretation, and known caveats.
ladder.minimum_evidence: Documented method, schema/tooling, or repeated comparable outputs.

ladder.meaning: Artifact is part of the stable Observatory language and can anchor other interpretations.
ladder.minimum_evidence: Promoted visitor-facing artifact, stable fixture contract, and clear non-ground-truth caveats.
